column2: A device that connects multiple devices in a LAN and forwards data based on MAC addresses.
A computer or other device connected to a network that provides or uses network services.
A device such as a computer or printer that is the source or destination of network messages.
A collection of devices connected to share resources and exchange data.
A computer that provides services or resources to other devices (clients) over a network.
A device or program that accesses services provided by a server.
A software application used to access and view websites and web content.
Occurring at the same time, such as multiple users accessing a network resource.
Small Office Home Office network designed for a small number of users or devices.
A server responsible for sending, receiving, and storing email messages.
A server that hosts websites and delivers web pages to users via HTTP/HTTPS.
A server that stores and manages access to files for users on a network.
A network model where devices share resources directly without a central server.
The ability of a network to grow and handle increasing demand without performance loss.
The efficiency and speed at which a network operates and delivers data.
Local Area Network, typically confined to a single building or campus.
Metropolitan Area Network, covering a city or large campus area.
Wide Area Network, spanning large geographic areas like countries or continents.
The tangible components of a network, like cables, switches, and routers.
The physical materials used to transmit data, such as copper, fiber, or wireless.
A network-provided function like file sharing, email, or web hosting.
Physical wire used to connect devices and transmit data in a network.
A device that forwards data between different networks and determines best path.
To be the foundation or base, such as protocols that underly network communication.
To start a network connection or data transfer.
The origin point where data is generated in a network.
The endpoint where data is delivered in a network.
The channel through which data travels, such as cable or wireless signal.
A set of rules that governs data communication between network devices.
A device like a router or switch that forwards data between end devices.
To produce or create data to be transmitted over the network.
To transmit data again, often after a transmission failure.
